pi_items.verified_value: VERIFIED - Verifier code line 294 confirms: passed = score >= 75
pi_items.source: verifier.py lines 292-294: score = int((criteria_met / total_criteria) * 100); passed = score >= 75

pi_items.metadata_value: Pass threshold of 75% requires at least 3 out of 4 criteria (from README)
pi_items.verified_value: VERIFIED - 3/4 * 100 = 75% confirms requirement for 3 out of 4 criteria

pi_items.metadata_value: Significant change threshold: >20 intensity units (from README and verifier.py line 98)
Target area modified when >=30% of target region changed (from README section A and verifier.py line 102)
Source-target similarity threshold: >=0.3 correlation (from verifier.py line 162)
Image meaningfully changed when >=1% of pixels modified (from verifier.py line 188)
Red marker detection: red lower bound [180, 0, 0] (from verifier.py line 32)
Red marker detection: red upper bound [255, 80, 80] (from verifier.py line 33)
Target area bounding box should be (x_min, y_min, x_max, y_max)
pi_items.verified_value: CONTRADICTED - CRITICAL BUG FOUND: verifier.py line 64 returns (x_min, y_min, x_max, y_min) with y_min appearing twice instead of y_max. This results in zero height bbox, causing analyze_cloning_changes to always return target_modified=False (lines 89-95), making successful clone verification impossible.
pi_items.source: verifier.py line 64: return {'bbox': (x_min, y_min, x_max, y_min), ...} should be (x_min, y_min, x_max, y_max)

privileged_info_summary: The clone_stamp@1 task uses GIMP's clone tool to copy texture from source areas to cover red-marked target regions. The task downloads landscape images from Unsplash (800x533 pixels), adds a 40x40 pixel red marker at coordinates (380,280) to (420,320), and verifies successful cloning using pixel analysis. CRITICAL: The verifier contains a severe bug on line 64 where the target bounding box is calculated as (x_min, y_min, x_max, y_min) instead of (x_min, y_min, x_max, y_max), resulting in zero height. This bug causes the 'target_modified' criterion to always return False (lines 89-95), making it impossible to pass verification even with correct cloning. The GIMP clone tool functionality itself is correctly described (Ctrl+click for source, C key to activate), and all other verification thresholds are correctly implemented (75% pass, 3/4 criteria required, >=30% target coverage, >=0.3 similarity, >=1% overall change).
